title: Cucumber sandwich
subtitle: Thin cucumber on buttered white bread, crusts removed
excerpt: The cucumber sandwich is the defining element of British afternoon tea. Thin slices of cucumber on very soft white bread spread with salted butter, with the crusts cut off. The restraint is the whole point: it is delicate, cool, and meant to be eaten in two bites.
sourceNotes: British afternoon tea tradition from the Victorian era. Cucumber sandwiches became associated with Edwardian upper-class tea parties and have remained the defining finger sandwich at British afternoon tea ever since. The combination of cool cucumber and buttered bread is one of the most recognised British food images.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble up to 1 hour ahead, cover tightly with a damp cloth, and keep chilled. Do not make more than an hour ahead or the bread becomes soggy.

content.text: The cucumber sandwich has two requirements: the cucumber must be sliced very thin, and the bread must be soft enough to roll slightly at the edge when pressed. Thick bread or thick cucumber makes a different sandwich entirely. Use a salted butter, not unsalted. The salt is part of the flavour. Some people add a thin scrape of white pepper; others add a little mint or cream cheese.

items.prepNote: very soft thin-cut white bread, or 1 small loaf
thinly sliced on a mandoline or with a sharp knife

content.text: Lay out all the bread slices. Spread the softened butter all the way to the edges on each slice.
Lay the cucumber slices in a single, slightly overlapping layer on half the bread slices, covering from edge to edge. Season lightly with salt and white pepper if using.
Press the remaining buttered slices on top. Cut off all four crusts with a sharp knife. Cut each sandwich into three fingers or four triangles.
Keep covered with a damp cloth or cling film and chill for up to 1 hour before serving.

items.cause: Cucumber held too much moisture, or made too far ahead.
items.fix: Salt the cucumber slices lightly on kitchen paper for 10 minutes and pat dry before assembling. Do not make more than 1 hour ahead.

items.cause: Unsalted butter was used, or cucumber was not seasoned.
items.fix: Use salted butter and add a small pinch of salt over the cucumber. The cucumber itself is mild and needs the salt.

items.symptom: The sandwiches fall apart when served
items.cause: Crusts not fully removed, or sliced too thick.
items.fix: A sharp knife and straight cuts give clean fingers. Remove all four crusts before cutting.

content.text: The cucumber sandwich came to prominence at Edwardian afternoon tea, where its delicacy suited the occasion. Oscar Wilde's characters eat them in The Importance of Being Earnest. They have never left the afternoon tea table. They remain the most recognisable finger sandwich in British catering.
